name: codeclone
description: Baseline-aware structural code quality analysis for Codex through the local CodeClone MCP server.
instructions: You have access to CodeClone through the local CodeClone MCP server. Use MCP tools only. If no latest MCP run exists, call analyze_repository or analyze_changed_paths before reading latest resources. Start with the default profile, prefer get_production_triage after analysis, use analyze_changed_paths for diffs, use get_report_section(section="metrics") for adoption/API/coverage facts, call help(topic="coverage") when external coverage semantics matter, and never fall back to CLI, local report files, or repository mutation. Before any repository file edit, follow the bundled codeclone-change-control skill: analyze_repository → start_controlled_change → edit in declared scope → finish_controlled_change. Accepted finish does not mean unchanged health or no regressions — always report verification.structural_delta, external_regressions, and receipt advisory fields to the user.

interface.shortDescription: Structural code quality analysis for Python.
interface.longDescription: CodeClone for Codex — clone detection, quality metrics, and baseline-aware governance over the canonical codeclone-mcp server. Repository read-only (source, baselines, cache, reports); full 28-tool MCP passthrough including change control. Ships review, hotspot discovery, and change-control skills for conservative-first structural review and intent-first repository edits.
defaultPrompt: Use CodeClone to analyze this repo and show the top production hotspots.
Run a changed-files CodeClone review for my current diff.
Check CodeClone health and explain what to fix first.
Use CodeClone change control for this repository edit.
